name: OverflowToolbar
description: A toolbar that moves items that do not fit into the available space into an overflow menu (three-dot button). Used as the table header toolbar on every SAP Fiori screen — contains the table title, ToolbarSpacer, and action buttons. Also used as the DynamicPage footer for draft-save actions.

props.name: design
props.description: Visual style of the toolbar. Auto adapts to context. Transparent removes the background. Use Auto (default) in most cases.

props.name: height
props.description: Toolbar height. Leave empty to use the default height determined by density (44px Cozy, 36px Compact).

props.name: active
props.description: If true, the toolbar has hover and press states. Use false (default) for non-interactive toolbars.

events.name: press
events.description: Fired when the toolbar is pressed. Only fires when active=true.

slots.name: content
slots.description: Toolbar content. Standard pattern: Title → ToolbarSpacer → action Buttons. Items that don't fit move to the overflow menu automatically.

notes: Always use OverflowToolbar (not sap.m.Toolbar) for table header toolbars — it handles overflow automatically.
Standard content order: Title (with item count) → ToolbarSpacer → action Buttons.
Place in Table headerToolbar slot for table actions, or in DynamicPage footer slot for Save/Discard.
Never put FilterBar inside OverflowToolbar — FilterBar belongs in DynamicPageHeader.

keyboard: Tab — move focus to next toolbar item
Left/Right arrow — move between toolbar items
notes: OverflowToolbar should have an aria-label when it cannot be labelled by an adjacent heading.
The overflow button (three dots) is automatically labelled 'More' by the framework.
Action buttons inside the toolbar must have visible text or a tooltip.

examples.title: Standard table header toolbar
whenToUse: As the headerToolbar of every sap.m.Table
As the footer of DynamicPage for draft save actions (Save + Discard)
whenNotToUse: Do not use sap.m.Toolbar instead — it has no overflow handling
Do not put FilterBar inside OverflowToolbar
